@@ -1349,7 +1349,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.17","title":"E2E: browser interaction scripts + detailed JSONL logging (FrankenTerm)","description":"Goal
- Add end-to-end browser interaction scripts that validate correctness AND produce high-signal logs.

Requirements
- Scripts cover: selection+copy, paste, OSC8 link click, resize storms, search, and long scrollback.
- Emit JSONL conforming to shared schema, including:
  - run_id, git_sha, browser, DPR, size
  - input counts + encoded bytes
  - frame hashes, patch sizes, and timing histograms

Acceptance Criteria
- Failures yield minimal repro traces that can be replayed deterministically.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:44.169687801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:13:26.682916113Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.17","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:44.169687801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.17","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:44.169687801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.17","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:44.169687801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.17","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:44.169687801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.17","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:44.169687801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.17","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:13:26.682856111Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.18","title":"Implement: Unicode/emoji rendering correctness (ZWJ, combining marks, wide chars)","description":"Goal
- Ensure FrankenTerm renders the full modern Unicode surface area with terminal-appropriate semantics.

Scope
- Wide glyphs, emoji sequences (ZWJ), variation selectors, combining marks.
- Fallback strategy when shaping is not available.

Testing
- Golden fixtures (strings -> expected cell layout) reused across core + web.
- Visual checksum tests for representative lines.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.19","title":"Optional: ligatures/shaping pipeline (Harfbuzz)","description":"Goal
- Support code-font ligatures and better complex-script shaping when it materially improves UX.

Acceptance Criteria
- Shaping is feature-gated and does not regress perf by default.
- Golden checks prove layout stability.

-{"id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","title":"Implement: new crate frankenterm-web (wasm-bindgen API + build plumbing)","description":"Goal
- Add `crates/frankenterm-web` with WASM bindings and a minimal JS-facing API.

Requirements
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.
- Expose: init(canvas_id or handle), resize, feed_input, present_patch.

Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check -p frankenterm-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` succeeds.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.20","title":"Optional: auto URL detection (non-OSC8) + safe click handling","description":"Goal
- Detect plain-text URLs in the viewport/scrollback and make them clickable (similar to xterm.js WebLinks addon).

Scope
- Heuristics tuned to avoid false positives in code.
- Security: sanitize and enforce allowed protocols.

Testing
- Fixture corpus for common URL forms.
- E2E: click opens expected target (or emits link event) with JSONL logs.
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:16.284093147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:16.284093147Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.20","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:16.284093147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.20","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:16.284093147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.20","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:16.284093147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","title":"Implement: WebGPU renderer skeleton (device init + background cell quads)","description":"Goal
- Get pixels on screen: render the terminal background + cell backgrounds at interactive speeds.

Requirements
- WebGPU init (adapter/device/queue) + resize handling.
- Draw pass for background and per-cell bg colors.
- Path for applying dirty-span patches only (no full redraw required).

Acceptance Criteria
- A minimal web harness can render a synthetic grid and resize without artifacts.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:52.352652196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:52.352652196Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:52.352652196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:52.352652196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:52.352652196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:52.352652196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","title":"Implement: glyph rasterization + atlas cache (monospace first)","description":"Goal
- Turn glyphs into atlas textures with stable metrics and minimal overhead.

Requirements
- Start with monospace ASCII + box drawing + common symbols; expand to full Unicode coverage via bd-lff4p.2.18.
- Deterministic glyph keys/ids across runs and platforms (within a given font build).
- Cache with explicit eviction policy + metrics (hit/miss, bytes, upload cost).
- Hot path: no allocations when glyph already cached.

Testing
- Unit tests for key stability and eviction invariants.
- Golden fixtures for a representative glyph set (metrics + atlas coordinates).
- Bench harness integration to measure cache miss cost and steady-state throughput.

Acceptance Criteria
- Benchable API: given a glyph key, returns atlas coords and advances with no allocations in hot path.
- Upload + packing strategy is measurable and regression-gated.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:51.394478179Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1371,7 +1371,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.9","title":"Optimize: wasm binary size + startup time (LTO, feature gating)","description":"Goal
- Make the web demo load fast and keep wasm artifacts small.

Acceptance Criteria
- Measured size targets with before/after numbers.
- Startup (time-to-first-frame) improved with profiling evidence.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:51.411482665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:51.411482665Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:51.411482665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:51.411482665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:53:51.411482665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4","title":"ftui-native backend: replace Crossterm (Unix-first)","description":"Scope
- Replace Crossterm with a first-party backend: raw mode, alt-screen, mouse, bracketed paste, focus events, kitty keyboard, resize, and robust cleanup.
- Drive input via ftui-core InputParser on raw bytes (no Crossterm event translation).

Deliverables
- New backend crate (name TBD) and integration into ftui-core/ftui-runtime.
- PTY + panic-cleanup tests; perf improvements via fewer syscalls + tighter buffering.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:31.341662263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:31.341662263Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:31.341662263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.1","title":"Implement: new native terminal backend crate (ftui-tty)","description":"Goal
- Add a first-party native terminal backend crate to replace Crossterm.

Scope
- Unix-first (Linux/macOS). Windows later.

Acceptance Criteria
- Minimal API compiles and can be used by ftui-core/ftui-runtime via a backend trait.
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` in the crate.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:12.192287374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:03:13.936810721Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:03:13.936792076Z","close_reason":"Implemented ftui-backend (traits) + ftui-tty (skeleton): BackendClock, BackendEventSource, BackendPresenter, Backend traits + TtyBackend impl with 8 tests. Unblocks bd-lff4p.4.2, 4.3, 4.4.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:12.192287374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:12.192287374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-lff4p.4.2","title":"Implement: Unix raw mode + terminal feature toggles (alt-screen, mouse, paste, focus, kitty)","description":"Goal
- Replace Crossterm terminal mode management with our own, with stronger semantics and fewer surprises.

Requirements
- Raw mode enter/exit.
- Alt screen enter/leave.
- Mouse capture enable/disable.
- Bracketed paste + focus events.
- Kitty keyboard protocol enable/disable.
- Robust Drop-based cleanup + panic safety.

Acceptance Criteria
- PTY tests prove cleanup on normal exit and panic.
- No leaked terminal state (cursor visible, raw mode off, alt-screen restored).
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:20.934040745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:20.934040745Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:20.934040745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:20.934040745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:20.934040745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-lff4p.4.2","title":"Implement: Unix raw mode + terminal feature toggles (alt-screen, mouse, paste, focus, kitty)","description":"Goal
- Replace Crossterm terminal mode management with our own, with stronger semantics and fewer surprises.

Requirements
- Raw mode enter/exit.
- Alt screen enter/leave.
- Mouse capture enable/disable.
- Bracketed paste + focus events.
- Kitty keyboard protocol enable/disable.
- Robust Drop-based cleanup + panic safety.

Acceptance Criteria
- PTY tests prove cleanup on normal exit and panic.
- No leaked terminal state (cursor visible, raw mode off, alt-screen restored).
","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:20.934040745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:04:37.950358977Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:20.934040745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:20.934040745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:20.934040745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.3","title":"Implement: Unix input reader (poll + bytes) feeding ftui-core InputParser","description":"Goal
- Replace Crossterm event polling with a tight byte-reader + ftui-core parsing.

Requirements
- Non-blocking or poll-based read with timeout.
- Convert bytes to ftui-core Event via existing InputParser.
- Support resize, focus, bracketed paste, mouse sequences.

Acceptance Criteria
- Input parity tests vs current behavior for core keys and mouse sequences.
- No busy-looping when idle.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:27.260346524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:27.260346524Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:27.260346524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:27.260346524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:27.260346524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.4","title":"Implement: resize detection + size queries (SIGWINCH)","description":"Goal
- Provide accurate, low-latency resize handling without Crossterm.

Requirements
- Query terminal size via ioctl.
- Deliver resize events via SIGWINCH or equivalent.

Acceptance Criteria
- Stress test: resize storm does not drop or reorder events; no panics.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:32.571651561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:32.571651561Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:32.571651561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:32.571651561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","title":"Integrate: swap ftui runtime to new backend (native path)","description":"Goal
- Wire the new native backend into `ftui-runtime` and remove all direct Crossterm coupling from the runtime hot path.

Non-Negotiable Compatibility
- Behavior must match current demo semantics (input, resize, mouse, paste, focus, kitty keyboard, alt-screen/inline rules).
- One-writer rule and terminal cleanup semantics must remain correct under normal exit and panic/unwind.

Testing + Logging
- Run the existing PTY E2E suite (`tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh` or curated subsets) against the new backend.
- E2E logs must validate against `tests/e2e/lib/e2e_jsonl_schema.json` and include backend identity + capability profile.
- Add/extend tests as needed to cover any behavior differences discovered during migration.

Acceptance Criteria
- `ftui-demo-showcase` runs on the new backend with identical user-visible behavior.
- E2E suite is green and produces detailed JSONL logs usable for regression triage.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:14:43.883488942Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1392,7 +1392,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.5","title":"Perf gates: baseline thresholds for parser MB/s and web frame times","description":"Goal
- Turn benchmarks into regression gates.

Acceptance Criteria
- Baselines captured and checked in.
- CI fails on significant regressions (with documented thresholds).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.6","title":"Alien Artifact: formal cost models for caches + scheduling (renderer + parser)","description":"Goal
- Use principled math to design cache eviction, batching, and scheduling with explicit objective functions.

Examples
- Explicit loss functions for cache misses vs memory.
- Queueing/scheduling model for input vs render vs present.
- Evidence ledger: why a policy was chosen, with measured priors/posteriors.

Acceptance Criteria
- Documented models + experiments that justify the final policies.
- Results integrated into code comments and perf docs.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.7","title":"Optional: differential testing against reference terminal emulators","description":"Goal
- Validate FrankenTerm correctness via differential testing.

Approach
- Feed identical output byte streams to multiple reference emulators.
- Compare resulting grid snapshots and cursor state to FrankenTerm.

Acceptance Criteria
- Differential mismatches become tracked fixtures with root-cause notes.

- Logs are detailed enough to diagnose a mismatch without reproducing locally.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.9","title":"QA: golden frame/pixel checksum pipeline for web renderer","description":"Goal
- Add a renderer-level golden system so we can prove visual correctness across refactors and optimizations.

Scope
- Define what constitutes a stable golden (framebuffer hash, patch hash, or both).
- Provide fixtures that render deterministic scenes and compute hashes.

Acceptance Criteria
- A mismatch reports: frame index, region summary, and reproduction trace id.
- Gates integrate with CI (time-bounded).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.6","title":"Spec: FrankenTerm + ftui-web end-to-end North Star architecture","description":"Goal
- Write the definitive, no-compromises system design for: FrankenTerm (terminal engine + renderer) + ftui-web runtime + native backend replacement.

Must Cover
- Layering boundaries (core vs web vs native).
- Data flows: input -> parse -> model update -> render -> present.
- Determinism strategy (record/replay, seeded demos, time sources).
- Safety constraints: safe Rust, panic discipline, no leaked terminal state.
- Performance budgets (p95/p99) and how we will measure them.

Acceptance Criteria
- Concrete module/crate map with responsibility boundaries.
- Explicit invariants + failure modes + rollback strategy.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:52.083552772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:33:54.203521839Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:33:54.203490831Z","close_reason":"Wrote comprehensive architecture spec at docs/spec/frankenterm-architecture.md covering: crate map (5 new crates), TerminalBackend trait, data flows for native/web/remote, determinism strategy, safety constraints, performance budgets, invariants, failure modes, rollback strategy, and phased implementation order mapped to existing beads","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:52.083552772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.7","title":"Process: Extreme Optimization loop for FrankenTerm and ftui-web","description":"Goal
- Make the Extreme Software Optimization loop mandatory across FrankenTerm + ftui-web (core engine, web renderer, native IO).

Mandatory Loop (every perf change)
1. BASELINE: capture p50/p95/p99 + memory; record run_id + git sha
2. PROFILE: CPU + allocations + (web) GPU timing when available
3. OPPORTUNITY MATRIX: Score = Impact x Confidence / Effort; implement only Score >= 2.0
4. IMPLEMENT: exactly one lever per commit; no behavior changes mixed in
5. PROVE: golden outputs + explicit isomorphism proof (ordering/ties/FP/RNG)
6. VERIFY: trace replay + sha256/checksum gates
7. REPEAT: re-profile (hotspots shift)

Deliverables
- Repeatable harnesses
  - Core parser/grid: `cargo bench -p frankenterm-core` + realistic byte-stream corpus
  - Web renderer: in-browser harness that emits machine-readable JSON (frame_time histogram, time_to_first_frame, steady-state memory)
  - Native backend: PTY harness + present-time metrics + syscall profile (e.g. `strace -c`)
- Golden artifact strategy
  - JSONL traces (reuse/extend `tests/e2e/lib/e2e_jsonl_schema.json`)
  - Frame/patch checksums stored in an append-only registry
- Templates
  - Opportunity matrix table + scoring rubric
  - Isomorphism proof template for every perf-related change
  - Regression threshold policy (when CI should fail)

Acceptance Criteria
- For each subsystem (core/web/native) we can run: baseline -> profile -> replay -> gate.
- Every optimization PR includes before/after numbers + a single-lever diff + isomorphism proof + golden verification.
","notes":"Drafted docs/profiling/extreme-optimization-loop.md (baseline/profile/opportunity matrix/one-lever/isomorphism proof/regression thresholds + harness sketches) and linked it from docs/operational-playbook.md.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMill","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:57.563856458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:40:57.929861284Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:40:57.929838291Z","close_reason":"Completed: added extreme optimization loop playbook + operational-playbook link","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:57.563856458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
